notes: David Davidar. His Other Books: “The solitude of emperors”, “Ithaca”, and “Nila amera kuṭhi”.

Gujarati. Kanhaiyalal Maneklal Munshi, popularly known by his pen name Ghanshyam Vyas, was an Indian independence movement activist, politician, writer from Gujarat state. Books by him: ‘’Krishnavatara II The Wrath Of An Emperor’’, ‘’Atta Ke Svapna’’, ‘’Gaurava K Pratka’’, ‘’Lopamudra’’, ‘‘Pratiodha’’, ‘’Akhand Hindustan’’, ‘’Gujarat and Its Literature’’, ‘’The Book of Satyabhama’’, ‘’The Ruin That Britain Wrought’’, ‘’Kakani Raashi Ane Chiye Tej Thik’’.

Tarabai Shinde was a feminist activist who protested patriarchy and caste in 19th century India. She is known for her published work, Stri Purush Tulana, originally published in Marathi in 1882. Pandita Ramabai wrote the Stree Dharma-Neeti (1882) book on the duties and moral obligations of women in India. Rassundari Devi wrote the autobiography 'Amar Jibon' (1876). Sarojini Naidu (first Governor of United Provinces) - The Village Song, The Golden Threshold, The Sceptred Flute.

Mirpore. In Custody (1984) is a novel set in Delhi by Anita Desai. Her other novels include “Journey to Ithaca”, “Clear Light of Day”, “Fasting, Feasting”, “The Zigzag Way”, and “The Peacock Garden”. Malgudi is a fictional town located in south India in the novels and short stories of R.K. Narayan. Lilliput fictional island nations that appear in the novel Gulliver's Travels by Jonathan Swift. His other works include “Nine Stories”, “Franny and Zooey”, “Raise High the Roof Beam”. HG Wells - “The War of the Worlds”, “The Time Machine”, “The Invisible Man”. Ernest Hemingway - “The Old Man and the Sea”, “The Sun Also Rises”. Mark Twain - “Adventures of Huckleberry Finn”, “The Innocents Abroad”.

Atmakatha. Anna Chandy was the first woman judge in India and the Commonwealth, appointed as a munsif in 1937. She later became the first female High Court judge in India in 1959. Other Autobiographies: Yuvraj Singh - ‘’The Test of My Life’’, Sachin Tendulkar - ‘’Playing it My Way’’, Milkha Singh - ‘’The Race of My Life’’. Q.1196. Who has written the book ‘Fearless Governance’, published in January 2021, describing her stint as Governor ? SSC CGL 11/09/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Draupadi Murmu (b) Baby Rani Maurya (c) Kiran Bedi (d) Anandi ben Patel Sol.1196.(c) Kiran Bedi. She is the first woman in India to join the officer ranks of the Indian Police Service (IPS) in 1972. Her other books: ‘It's always possible’, ‘Himmat Hai’, ‘Dare to Do! For the New Generation’.

MK Stalin. Other Chief ministers’ books : Mamata Banerjee - ‘My Unforgettable Memories’, Yogi Adityanath - ‘Hathyoga : Swaroop evam Sadhna’, Arvind Kejriwal - ‘Swaraj’. Q.1198. Who has written the book Sangeet Bal Prakash? SSC CGL 25/09/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Pandit Narayan Rao (b) Vishnu Digambar Paluskar (c) Vishnu Narayan Bhatkhande (d) Bhimsen Joshi Sol.1198.(b) Vishnu Digambar Paluskar founded the Gandharva Mahavidyalaya in Lahore in 1901. His notable works include Baalbodh (Children's Knowledge) in five volumes, Raag Pravesh (Entry into Raagas) in nineteen volumes, and Mahilaa Sangeet (Ladies' Music). Paluskar also innovated musical performances through enterprises like Tablaa Tarang (Tabla Vibrations) and Kaashtha Tarang (Wood Vibrations).

Sekhar Bandyopadhyay. His other Book: ‘’Caste and Partition in Bengal: The Story of Dalit Refugees, 1946-1961’’. Romila Thapar - ‘’Somanatha’’, ‘’Sakuntala: Texts, Readings, Histories’’. Devid Hardiman - ‘’Gandhi in His Time and Ours: The Global Legacy of His Ideas’’, ‘’Feeding the Baniya: Peasants and Usurers in Western India’’. Sumit Sarkar - ‘’Writing Social History’’, ‘’The Swadeshi Movement in Bengal, 1903-1908’’.

Sangeeta Sudhakara. The term ‘Carnatic’ and ‘Hindustani’ to classify musical styles first appeared in it. The Gita Govinda is a work composed by the 12th-century Hindu poet, Jayadeva. Sangeet Darpan is a musical treatise on Indian classical music written by Damodar Pandit. The Sangita-Ratnakara (Ocean of Music and Dance) is one of the most important musicological texts from India. Q.1201. The famous book 'Sangraha Choodamani', which deals with the current system of Carnatic music was written by: SSC CHSL 03/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Damodara Misra (b) Haripala (c) Matanga (d) Govindacharya Sol.1201.(d) Govindacharya. Matanga Muni authored the Brihaddesi, a significant text in the study of Indian music. Bharata Muni is traditionally attributed with the Natya Shastra, an ancient musical and dramatic treatise.

Famous Novels by him- ‘Gadh Kundar’, ‘Virata ki Padmini’ ‘Musahibju’, ‘Jhansi ki Rani’, ‘Kachnar’, ‘Madavji Sindhia’,’Tute Kante’, ‘Bhuvan Vikram’, ‘Ahilya Bai’ etc.

Ernest Hemingway - ‘The Old Man and the Sea’, ‘A Farewell to Arms’ etc. William Faulkner - ‘A Fable’, ‘The Reivers’ etc. Roald Dahl- ‘Matilda’, ‘Charlie and the Chocolate Factory’ etc. Q.1354. Which of the following autobiographies is written by former English footballer, David Beckham published in 2003 ? SSC CHSL 10/06/2022 (Evening) (a) A Shot at History (b) The Greatest: My own Story (c) My Side (d) Standing my Ground Sol.1354.(c) ‘My Side’. Personality and their autobiography: Muhammad Ali - ‘The Greatest: My own Story’, Mathew Hayden - ‘Standing My Ground’.

Shri Narendra Modi. Books by Shri Narendra Modi - ‘Exam Warriors’, ‘Abode of Love’, ‘Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas’, ‘Nayan he Dhanya Re’, ‘Sangharsh Ma Gujarat’, ‘Sakshibhav’, ‘Aapatkal Mein Gujarat’, ‘Mann ki Baat’, etc.

Devdutt Pattanaik. More famous books by him are ‘Jaya’, ‘My Gita’, ‘Krishna's Secret’, ‘7 Secrets of Shiva’, ‘Devlok with Devdutt Pattanaik’, ‘How To Take Decisions’, ‘Sita’, ‘My Hanuman Chalisa’, ‘How to become Rich’. Q.1357. Who among the following science fiction/ popular science writers wrote ‘2001 : A Space Odyssey’ and held the position of a Professor at the Physical Research Laboratory, Ahmedabad? SSC CGL 18/04/2022 (Evening) (a) Isaac Asimov (b) Arthur C Clarke (c) Jules Verne (d) HG Wells Sol.1357.(b) Arthur C Clark - ‘Rama Series’, 'Childhood Ends’, ‘City and the Stars’, ‘Fountains of Paradise’.
Jaimini. Author and books: Charak - ‘Charaka Samhita’, Badarayana - ‘Brahma Sutras’.
Pranab Mukherjee - ‘The Dramatic Decade: The Indira Gandhi Years’, ‘The Turbulent Years’, ‘Thoughts and Reflections’.
Hiuen Tsiang. Author and books: Marco Polo - “The Description of the World '', “Travels in the Land of Kubilai Khan ''.
APJ Abdul Kalam. Books - “Wings of Fire”, “Ignited Minds”, “Turning Points”.
My Truth. Her other books - “Freedom’s Daughter”, "Remembered Moments".
Margaret Atwood. ‘The Testaments’ is a sequel to ‘The Handmaid Tales’. The Testaments was joint winner of the 2019 Booker Prize, alongside Bernardine Evaristo's novel “Girl, Woman, Other”.
Roald Dahl. Lewis Carroll : ‘Alice's Adventures in Wonderland’ (1865) and ‘Through the Looking-Glass’, and ‘What Alice Found There’ (1872).
JK Rowling. Other novels: “Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone”, “The Ink Black Heart’’, “Troubled Blood”.
P Chidambaram. Other books: “Fearless in Opposition”, “Standing Guard”, Undaunted - “Saving the Idea of India”.
Nand Kishore Acharya. Other famous books by him- ‘Sanskriti ka Vyakarna’, ‘Sabhyata ka Vikalp’, ‘Gulam Badshah’, etc. He was awarded Sahitya Akademi Award 2019 in Hindi literature.

Paro Anand. She won the Sahitya Akademi Bal Sahitya Puraskar in 2017 for her anthology Wild Child and Other Stories (now published as "Like Smoke: 20 Teens 20 Stories''). Q.1369. ‘Bhavartha Dipika’ and ‘Amritanubhava’ are the sacred books of the people of ______. SSC MTS 27/10/2021 (Evening) (a) Haryana (b) Gujarat (c) Maharashtra (d) Rajasthan Sol.1369.(c) Maharashtra. The author of the book ‘Bhavartha Dipika’ and ‘Amritanubhava’ is Sant Dyaneshwar.

Calcutta (Kolkata). Q.1371. Willy Wonka is a fictional character from the children's story book '______'. SSC MTS 02/11/2021 (Morning) (a) Alice's Adventures in Wonderland (b) Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (c) Matilda (d) Charlotte's Web Sol.1371.(b) Charlie and the Chocolate Factory written by Roald Dahl. Charlotte's Web (E. B White), Matilda (Roald Dahl).

William Dalrymple. More famous books by him are- ‘Kohinoor: The Story of the World’s Most Infamous Diamond’, ‘Return of a King’, ‘The Last Mughal’, ‘Nine Lives’, ‘White Mughals’, ‘From the Holy Mountain’.
Manmohan Singh. ‘Changing India’ was written by him.
Jairam Ramesh. The Light of Asia is a biblio-biography of Edwin Arnold’s palpably fresh grasp of a complex philosophy and a worthy contribution to modern Buddhist studies.

Satya Nadella. “Hit Refresh: The Quest to Rediscover Microsoft's Soul and Imagine a Better Future for Everyone” is a nonfiction book by Satya Nadella and co-authors Jill Tracie Nichols and Greg Shaw, with a foreword by Bill Gates, published in 2017.
Swami Vivekananda. Other books - ‘Meditation and Its Methods’, ‘Jnana Yoga’, ‘Work and Its Secret’, ‘Thoughts of Power’.
Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan. Other books - ‘The Hindu view of life’, ‘The Pursuit of Truth’.

Rajendralala Mitra. More books by him are ‘The Chandogya Upanishad’, ‘Antiquities Of Orissa’, ‘The Sanskrit Buddhist literature of Nepal’ etc. HH Cole - appointed as the Curator of Ancient Monuments in 1880.
Vidiadhar Surajprasad Naipaul. Other novels - ‘A House for Mr Biswas’, ‘A Bend in the River’, ‘Miguel Street’, ‘In a Free State’.
Mahasweta Devi-‘Imaginary Maps’, ‘Hazaar Chaurasi ki Maa,’ ‘Breast Stories’, ‘Chotti Munda and His Arrow’, ‘Five Plays’, etc.
